description: Harness taut, a horse steps forward and the wagon follows. 'natu' draws something towards the puller or along a held connection. The wagon shows why distance alone cannot define the verb: it travels with the horse without coming up to its body. 'pesa' (push) sends force away from the pressing contact.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and open into stressed central 'a'. Return the tongue tip to the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't', release it, then raise the back of the tongue and round the lips for high-back 'u'.
sound_symbolism: The voiced dental nasal lets 'na' run without an oral release. The tongue then closes for 't' and the lips round around final 'u', so the form gathers from an open vowel into a compact ending.
usage_notes: The object under tension stands before the verb. Directional phrases can name its path or destination, while manner and duration distinguish a tug from a sustained draw. 'natu' also names one pull as an event noun.

semantic_domains.activity: exerting force through a drawing action or held connection
semantic_domains.physical: force that draws a body or object along its line of contact
semantic_domains.spatial: movement towards a puller or along an attached course
